title: Project invariants into a configurable doc target
description:
Render the invariant registry into a configurable documentation target so the declared invariants stay reflected in human-readable docs. Two modes are supported: a delimited region within an existing file, and a separate jit-owned file. The shipped default targets a separate jit-owned file so the default behavior never touches existing docs. The target path comes only from config; the invariant-projection code path contains no documentation-filename literal.

Region mode byte-preserves all content outside the delimiters; separate-file mode writes atomically. Writes go through a shared atomic writer extracted as a public `write_file_atomic` from the currently-private `write_atomic` (`crates/jit/src/validation/serialize.rs:180`); the extraction updates its single existing caller in the same change. (The grep for hardcoded filenames is scoped to the new projection path, since unrelated pre-existing literals exist at `crates/jit/src/document/link_validator.rs:188` and `crates/jit/src/commands/snapshot.rs:562`.)

## Success Criteria

- [hard] REQ-01: In region mode, only the delimited region is rewritten and content outside the delimiters is byte-preserved.
- [hard] REQ-02: In separate-file mode, the target file is written atomically.
- [hard] REQ-03: The shipped default config targets a separate jit-owned file.
- [hard] REQ-04: The invariant-projection code path contains no documentation-filename literal; the target path is read only from config (grep scoped to that path).
- [hard] REQ-05: Projection writes go through the shared atomic writer; the extracted `write_file_atomic`'s single prior caller is updated and remains green under `cargo test`.
